channel.title: Unlicensed Therapy w/ Ari Mannis
channel.description: Ari Mannis gives unsolicited advice to his favorite friends and comedians.
channel.author: Ari Mannis
channel.itunes:author: Ari Mannis
channel.itunes:summary: Ari Mannis gives unsolicited advice to his favorite friends and comedians.

item.title: Ophelia Faye on Life in the Adult Industry, Dealbreaker Hobbies & Caregiver Boundaries
item.description: Ari sits down with adult performer Ophelia Faye for a wide-ranging conversation that earns its "unlicensed therapy" label. Ophelia breaks down the difference between a performer and a "star," why she got into the industry in the first place, and how independent platforms have shifted the power dynamic for creators post-#MeToo.From there it gets personal: the studies that quietly push purity culture, the case for fantasy and stress relief, her long-game career strategy, and what a supportive agent actually does. Then Ari opens the listener mailbag — partners with questionable hobbies, the multivitamin smuggling operation that saved a relationship, and the harder one: how to set boundaries when family expects you to become a full-time caregiver.Follow Ophelia on Instagram and her other platforms none of this is real therapy. Probably.

item.title: Let’s Talk Love & Dating | Sabrina Zohar
item.description:
In Episode 180 of Unlicensed Therapy, Ari Mannis sits down with Sabrina Zohar for a conversation centered on love, dating, and online dating. The episode also features questions from the audience, leading to candid, wide-ranging discussions and real-time advice.

Together, Ari and Sabrina explore modern dating experiences, navigating relationships, and the realities of meeting people online, while responding directly to audience questions throughout the episode.

item.title: Fast Food & Felonies: Ian Bick Gets Unlicensed
item.description: Ari sits down with Ian Bick to talk about his wild journey from jail to YouTube fame — using an illegal phone behind bars, sneaking in fast food, prison wrestling matches, and filming with MrBeast. Plus, the two give unlicensed advice to listeners who write in with their real-life problems.

item.title: From Basement to Big City: Therapy Gecko Gets Unlicensed
item.description: Ari sits down with Therapy Gecko for a raw, hilarious, and surprisingly deep conversation. The two give unlicensed advice to write ins, talk about Gecko’s rise from his mom’s basement to LA and now NYC, the weird world of internet fame, stand-up comedy, dating, and what it really means to put yourself out there online.

item.title: Ari & Lenoci: Gay for Each Other? | Dating Shows & Wild Advice
item.description: This week on Unlicensed Therapy, Ari Mannis sits down with comedian Michael Lenoci to answer unfiltered advice questions from listeners. The two dive into Michael’s new dating show, Ari’s stand-up set (which Michael finally watched), and whether these two men might secretly be in love with each other. It’s chaos, honesty, and comedy at its finest — therapy the way it should be: completely unlicensed.

item.title: Biohacking, Weight Loss & Semen Retention with Dave Asprey
item.description: On this episode of Unlicensed Therapy, Ari sits down with biohacking pioneer Dave Asprey to discuss his upcoming book, his latest ventures, and the science behind optimizing your body and mind. Dave shares his insights on weight loss, longevity, and why ejaculating less might just be the key to unlocking peak performance. Tune in for an unfiltered conversation that’s as enlightening as it is hilarious.

item.title: Comedy, Growth & Dating Advice with Francisco Ramos
item.description: On this episode of Unlicensed Therapy, Ari is joined by the hilarious Francisco Ramos to dive into the world of stand-up, personal growth, and relationships. They take advice from listeners, share insights on the comedy grind, and get real about dating and women. A mix of laughs, wisdom, and unfiltered conversation—don’t miss it!

item.title: Comedy, Chaos, and Fatherhood with Mo Mandel
item.description: In this engaging episode of Unlicensed Therapy, host Ari Mannis sits down with comedian Mo Mandel for a candid conversation. They delve into the challenges of handling angry audience members, share personal stories about Mo's journey into fatherhood, and offer unfiltered advice to listener-submitted questions. Additionally, Mo promotes his latest comedy special, Trying To Make It, now available for streaming. Don't miss this blend of humor and heartfelt discussion!

item.title: Todd Glass & Lucas Hirl
item.description: In this hilarious episode of Unlicensed Therapy, host Ari Mannis welcomes comedy legends Todd Glass and Lucas Hirl! The trio dives into Panda Express's new Hot Ones Chicken collaboration, giving their spicy takes on this tasty treat. Todd shares insights from his viral TikToks and teases his upcoming comedy special, while Lucas chimes in with his unique perspective.But that's not all! They also tackle fan-submitted questions, offering their no-holds-barred advice on love, life, and everything in between. Whether you're craving laughs or seeking wisdom, this episode has it all!Tune in for a blend of comedy and candid conversations that only Unlicensed Therapy can deliver. Don’t miss it!

item.title: Pauly Shore & Mat Edgar
item.description: In this hilarious and heartfelt episode of Unlicensed Therapy, host Ari Mannis is joined by comedy legends Pauly Shore and Mat Edgar. The trio dives into the story of how Ari first met Pauly, leading to a whirlwind journey on the road together. Pauly shares his experiences of helping the younger generation of comedians find their footing in the industry, while Mat adds his own unique perspective on the ups and downs of comedy life. The episode wraps up with the three comedians dishing out unfiltered advice to listeners who wrote in, blending humor with genuine wisdom. Whether you're a comedy fan or just in need of a good laugh, this episode is a must-listen!

item.title: Orion Levine
item.description:
In this episode of Unlicensed Therapy, host Ari Mannis is joined by fellow stand-up comedian Orion Levine. The two met at the Big Pine Comedy Festival in San Diego, and their shared experiences in the comedy scene make for a lively and candid conversation.

Ari kicks things off by sharing a recent incident where he upset an audience member, leading to a negative Google review. They delve into the challenges of handling criticism as a comedian, balancing the need for creative expression with audience expectations.

The conversation takes a sharp turn as they discuss the shocking news of an assassination attempt on President Trump and the political shake-up following Biden's decision to drop out of the race. With Kamala Harris stepping in as his potential successor, Ari and Orion offer their unfiltered thoughts on the state of American politics.

But it wouldn’t be Unlicensed Therapy without some real talk. Orion opens up about his own personal struggles, and the duo takes time to read and respond to audience-submitted issues, dishing out advice with their signature blend of humor and honesty.

Tune in for a rollercoaster of emotions, insights, and laughs as Ari and Orion navigate the ups and downs of life, comedy, and everything in between.

item.title: Hanna Dickinson
item.description:
In this hilarious and unfiltered episode of Unlicensed Therapy, comedian Ari Mannis sits down with the incredibly funny Hanna Dickinson. The two dive into the ups and downs of Ari's recent breakup, exploring the emotional rollercoaster that comes with ending a relationship.

They also tackle the age-old question: Are six-pack abs really the key to a man's attractiveness? Listen in as Ari and Hanna share their candid thoughts and hilarious anecdotes on body image and dating in the modern world.

As always, Ari and his guest offer advice to listeners who wrote into the show with their personal dilemmas. From relationship troubles to career advice, no topic is off-limits. However, be warned—some of the humor and advice might just tow the line with political correctness, including moments that brush up against controversial topics.

Tune in for a raw, honest, and side-splitting conversation that you won't want to miss. Whether you're looking for a good laugh, some unlicensed therapy, or just a break from your day, this episode has it all!

item.title: Sara Weinshenk
item.description:
Comedian Ari Mannis invites the insightful comedian Sara Weinshenk for a deep dive into the world of hypothetical situations and relationship dynamics. Joined by Ari's little brother Jonah and his friend Will Pepper, the studio becomes a hub for candid discussions and genuine exchanges.

Throughout the episode, Ari and Sara explore various hypothetical scenarios, from the bizarre to the thought-provoking, offering their unique perspectives and engaging in lively debates. With Jonah and Will chiming in with their own viewpoints, the conversation takes unexpected turns, revealing hidden insights and comedic gems along the way.

In addition to their hypothetical musings, Ari and Sara take on the role of relationship consultants as they delve into real people's relationship dilemmas. With empathy and honesty, they dissect the issues at hand, offering practical advice and heartfelt reflections to help navigate the complexities of love and connection.

Tune in for an engaging blend of hypotheticals, relationship wisdom, and genuine camaraderie that promises to leave you both entertained and enlightened.

item.title: Kevin Langue
item.description: Comedian Ari Mannis sits down with the internet's comedic sensation, Kevin Langue, for an exclusive and hilarious deep dive into the world of viral comedy. Join Ari as he unravels the story behind the uproarious content that has taken social media by storm. In this episode, Kevin Langue, the mastermind behind the side-splitting videos that have racked up millions of views, opens up about his journey into the world of comedy. Get ready to laugh out loud as Ari and Kevin delve into the influences that shaped Kevin's unique brand of humor. From the early inspirations to the modern-day comedic geniuses, discover the comedic roots that fuel Kevin's creativity and wit. But it's not all just laughs! Explore the backstory of this social media maverick as Kevin shares insights into his background, revealing the trials, triumphs, and unexpected turns that paved the way to his meteoric rise in the digital comedy sphere. Get ready for an engaging and uproarious conversation that peels back the layers of internet stardom, offering listeners an exclusive peek behind the curtain of viral comedy success. Tune in as Ari Mannis brings you this unfiltered, entertaining, and insightful conversation with Kevin Langue, the social media powerhouse whose comedic prowess knows no bounds.

item.title: Liam Cullagh
item.description:
In this episode, host Ari Mannis engages in an insightful conversation with Liam Cullagh, a prominent figure at Friday Bears, a dynamic sketch comedy company. Together, they offer listeners a window into the mechanics of sketch comedy, the inner workings of Friday Bears, and even conjure up an intriguing movie concept.

Liam Cullagh, with his wealth of experience, provides a candid view of the journey that Friday Bears has undertaken. From inception to execution, he sheds light on the sweat and dedication that go into creating hilarious sketches that resonate with audiences.

The highlight of the episode arrives when Ari and Liam collaborate to formulate a movie idea that encapsulates their creative synergy. Weaving in elements of humor and imagination, they sculpt a narrative featuring captivating characters and comedic scenarios that promise to captivate.

Apart from the creative process, Liam shares valuable insights for emerging comedians and sketch comedy enthusiasts. His practical advice and perspective offer a valuable guide for those looking to venture into the world of laughter-inducing performances.

In a surprising turn, Liam takes the opportunity to seek advice from Ari Mannis, showcasing the reciprocity of learning and growth even for seasoned professionals.

Join Ari Mannis and Liam Cullagh in this episode as they delve into the artistry behind Friday Bears, delve into the nuances of sketch comedy, and provide a glimpse into the sheer dedication that defines the comedy industry.

item.title: Chad Kroeger & JT Parr
item.description:
On today's episode, Chad Kroeger and JT Parr join me to talk about going on a trip to China, how the bros would stop the war and what celebrities deserve forgiveness.  Chad & JT have been around the open mic scene in Los Angeles for years, which is where we met but they have started to gain mainstream attention over past few years with many viral videos on social media.

item.title: Jeremy Piven
item.description: For this installment of Unlicensed Therapy, I welcome Jeremy Piven. Jeremy gives us a look behind the curtain of a thespian upbringing and shares his transition from actor to the stand-up stage. He lets us in on the worst bombing he’s experienced on stage along with the reveal of why he really dropped out of Speed-the-Plow.

item.title: Jessimae Peluso
item.description: This week on Unlicensed Therapy, I am joined by comedian and host of the new Netflix show Tattoo Redo, Jessimae Peluso. In this episode, Jessimae reveals to us her brief encounter with a man she believed to be a median during her most vulnerable state after her father’s passing. We follow that up by discussing the age-old question of what happens after we die? We also get into the release of Bill Cosby and speculate on his future plans on touring and performing.

item.title: Kassem G
item.description:
Today on Unlicensed Therapy, I am joined by comedian and host of the podcast Pajama Pants, Kassem Gharaibeh. Kassem is most notable for his YouTube channel, which has hosted popular web series like Going Deep and California On.
In this episode, Kassem and I get into one of our personal favorite topics, UFOs. We discuss the infamous Phoenix Lights, and I share my own sighting from my time in Roswell while Kassem reveals to us how his fascination with UFOs came to be. We also unpack his transition into G4tv and we discover the reason why he suddenly stopped posting on YouTube.

item.dc:creator: Ari Mannis
item.itunes:summary: Bobby takes a break from recording "It's The Bearded Man" podcast to come on Unlicensed Therapy and give Ari some motivation. He asks Ari about how to get his podcast to the next level.

item.title: Blair Socci
item.description:
Today on Unlicensed Therapy, I am joined by comedian Blair Socci. Blair is the host of the live show Dear Owen Wilson, where comedians read letters to celebrities they love—inspired by Blair’s very own letter written to Owen Wilson after a suicide attempt in 2007.
In this episode, Blair and I get into dating in your 30s and dating with intention. She reveals her motive behind this being the result of ongoing commitment issues with men. We also find out about Blair’s short but paramount pro volleyball career while at UCLA. Though it reached its end after an injury, this made room for the many projects and ideas Blair wanted to pursue.

item.title: Chad Kroeger aka (Chad Goes Deep)
item.description: This week on Unlicensed Therapy, I am joined by co-host of the podcast Going Deep with Chad and JT, Chad Kroeger. Chad and I get into the daddy drama going on with Kevin Fard and JT, and we discuss what it means to be an alpha and who deserves that title. Chad also lets us in on his recent heartbreak, but we later alleviate that sorrow by painting a fantasy of proposing to his dream girl while in space – though if that doesn’t work out, he can always counter that by being the first to do meth in space. We also touch on Chad’s skin regimen and his enthusiasm for surfing.

item.title: Mark Normand
item.description: Today on Unlicensed Therapy, I interview comedian and the host of Tuesdays with Stories! and We Might Be Drunk, Mark Normand. In this episode, Mark and I discuss the virulent nature of comparing yourself to your peers in the comedy industry but also juxtaposing the difference in what that looks like between the east and west coast. With that, Mark confesses the pressure of feeling the need to represent New York while in LA. We also get into the best ways to slide into a girls dm’s, emphasizing adult entertainers, and we explore the best course of action to reach a variety of audiences when shooting a special.

item.title: Nick Davis
item.description: Today on Unlicensed Therapy, I am joined by the producer of This Past Weekend and King and the Sting, Nick Davis. In this episode, Nick walks us through his journey from working a minimum wage job in Minneapolis to then living in his Jetta in LA and now producing some of the most successful podcasts in the industry; on top of that, running high stakes MMA gambling and predictions. Mark also shares a couple of anecdotes on getting laid off from Funny or Die and getting fired from Adam Corolla.

item.title: William Montgomery
item.description:
Today on Unlicensed Therapy I am joined by comedian William Montgomery. William is most famously known for his role on the Kill Tony podcast and is now in the works of creating his own podcast.
On this episode, William and I share soul-crushing experiences in college caused by not students, but professors. After Dr. Marler states that William is simply not cut out for History, he is crushed and eventually drops out of grad school killing his teaching career before it even started. The silver lining for William was learning that he never even loved history all that much and was soon able to discover, and rediscover, his true interests, like comedy and poetry… and Herbie the Lovebug.

item.title: Chelcie Lynn (Trailer Trash Tammy)
item.description:
Today on Unlicensed Therapy, I am joined by comedian, actress, and YouTuber Chelcie Lynn. Chelcie rose to fame through her Vine acting as her alter ego Trailer Trash Tammy. In this episode, Chelcie and I discuss her rise to fame using unconventional channels like YouTube. Although she referred to herself as only an “internet comedian”, Chelcie had the foresight to know that her success and fame were imminent. Fast forward 15 years.
To get to her level of celebrity Chelcie had to first get through the trenches of her childhood. As the oldest of three siblings, Chelcie found herself being the matriarch of the family. Her dad being an alcoholic and mom a meth addict, they often would disappear leaving no food only to return days later bringing chaos and fear. Throughout the mayhem, Chelcie was still able to overcome adversity and is now in a 15-year long marriage, has over 1 million followers on all social media platforms, and is on her way to becoming a sex icon!

item.title: Ali Macofsky
item.description: The episodes starts off with a McRib Mukbang then I interview comedian and host of the podcast “Resting Bitch Face”, Ali Macofsky. On this episode Ali seeks advice on how to set boundaries in the early stages of dating. Having only been in short and zealous relationships in the past the idea of setting boundaries sounded foreign and intimidating. On the rare occasion a relationship was beginning to show signs of something more serious an unknown feeling of fear would take over and causes Ali to search for flaws to then use it as means to end the relationship. As she continued the cycle of ending things too quickly and mixing lust with love a sex coach was serendipitously recommended to her. This sex coach ended up giving her one key factor Ali had not taken into consideration before, sex compatibility. Learning this proved to be essential and changes the way she sought out partners completely. We end of the show on a high note when Ali reveals to us how she got her first job working for On Air with Ryan Seacrest. It started as curiosity of just wanting to know how her voice sounded over the radio. Young Ali called into the station with no plan on what to say but with a vision of Britney Spears tickets. After singing toxic she wins tickets to American idol, the next day Ali calls back to grumble about not having VIP seating. Following that a producer got in contact with her dad for an idea for a show where she makes prank phone calls. Only after recording a few successful episodes Ali was offered a job that went on for the next 4 years!

item.title: Lara Beitz
item.description: On this installment of Unlicensed Therapy, I welcome comedian Lara Beitz. You’ve seen her on Comedy Central for Lights Out with David Spade as both a panelist and correspondent. In this episode, I come to Lara’s aid on approaching dating as a female comedian who often encounters intimidation from the opposite sex. We get into setting up a positive mindset for dating in preparation for playing the numbers game. We also touch on our tennis skills, and I break down why being attacked at gunpoint would be better than stabbed knifepoint.

item.title: Ron Taylor
item.description:
Today on Unlicensed Therapy I am joined by comedian Ron Taylor. Ron started pursuing comedy in his hometown of Detroit and shares with us how the inception of comedian came to be. In 2009 he started watching stand up clips on Youtube and researching comics without realizing there was a whole business behind comedy. Upon finding a Dave Chappelle clip where he states that being funny is a marketable skill Ron immediately switched his major from engineering to theatre art and got to work into learning everything comedy.
On this episode Ron give us some insight on doing comedy in Detroit, a city he considers to be the only bottom top tier black city, being a place with no pipeline into Hollywood unlike its counterpart cities Chicago and Atlanta. The only avenue into Hollywood from Detroit is to get out of Detroit. While in Detroit Ron reveals to us how his 1of 2 DUI’s came to be and how not even a lawyer would keep him from experiencing the disparity in sentencing from the strictest judge in Michigan. 30 days followed by 1-year probation was the final verdict. While spending those days in jail Ron learns that most inmates shared a common thread other than being black, all were over sentenced by petty “crimes”. Knowing that parking tickets, domestic disputes, and even child support could land you days to months in jail came the realization that black oppression is the fuel source of America. In a country where there must be a group of people as #2 in order for there to be a group of people to feel superior comes the idea that their fuel source is lives off of repression and possibly the only way to change that is to replace that source. So how do you change that? Stay with us as Ron shares with us some theories here on Unlicensed Therapy!

item.title: Tony Hinchcliffe
item.description: Today on Unlicensed Therapy I am joined by Tony Hinchcliffe. Tony is the host of the podcast “Kill Tony” and has written for shows like Comedy Central Roast. On this episode Tony shares with us the bridges he’s burned in his career, one being one of, if not the only, company that produce specials for Netflix and Amazon prime. What started as having a diva moment about not wanting to perform in front of an audience made up of mainly of mentally disabled people turned into cutting off a major connection in the industry. As we continue on with the theme of disappointment, Tony shares with us an anecdote of a wild night with Ted Sarandos. The night ended in a handshake regarding filming of future episodes of Kill Tony for Netflix. Days go by with no calls returned and Tony eventually learns that not only this prospect was not going to happen but that it this has also happened to other artists. As we reach the end of the podcast Tony and I get political. We juxtapose Trumps presidency along with his policy on Covid and wrap it up with each of our take on racism in America. Stay with us as we delve deeper here on Unlicensed Therapy.

item.title: Sam Tripoli 2
item.description: Today on Unlicensed Therapy I interviewed Sam Tripoli; he is a comedian and host of the podcast “Tin Foil Hat”. On this episode Sam and I talk conspiracy theories, he gives us a taste of a few popular theories that are, in his opinion, the most digestible ones. Starting with JFK assassination followed by 9/11. But before we get into that Sam shares with us how the inception of his podcast came to be. At the precipice of Hilary, Bernie, and the breakdown of exit polls from Jimmy Dore and Lee Camp there was what seemed to be an error in percentage amount needed for there to be voter fraud. Once he dug deep enough the idea of a podcast for conspiracy theories occurred and after an idiosyncratic title later the show snowballed into success. Towards the end of the podcast we discussed diversity in bookings, woke comedy, and downloading porn on LimeWire. Stay with us for more here on Unlicensed Therapy!

item.title: Matt Rife
item.description:
In this episode, we hear from Matt Rife. At the age of 15, Matt started his comedy career, and has since performed in global tours with some of the largest names in comedy, including Erik Griffin, Dane Cook, Deray Davis, Finesse Mitchell, Mike Epps, Ralphie May, and more. Since moving to LA, Matt regularly performs at the renowned Laugh Factory. His comedy has allowed him to transition into acting, and his break came as a leading cast member on MTV’s Wild ‘N Out.
Today, he talks about his uncomfortable financial situation, being known as ‘the 17-year-old at Laugh Factory, having bad drug trips, and dealing with haters.
What is Matt’s biggest disappointment? Why does he want to give up comedy? Ari tells us about his dream of owning a custom camper van, but Matt plants some doubt in the bathroom situation, and we hear about the raunchy dramedy movie that Matt is working on. This, and so much more, on this episode of Unlicensed Therapy.

item.title: Tim Dillon
item.description:
Today on Unlicensed Therapy I am joined by comedian, writer, and actor Tim Dillon. Tim shares with us a bit of his backstory on how he went from mortgage broker during the housing crisis to then reaching a tipping point where he was faced with a number of underlying self-revelations that directed him to pursue a career in comedy. While working on getting recognition to his name Tim credits the majority of his fan base to podcasting and comedian peers. Fast forward to present day he has gained over 200k subscribers for his podcast on YouTube and has been featured on two major media platforms for his stand-up.
In this episode we cover the festishization of mental illness and how over diagnosed it has become, partly as a result of people looking for an identity and finding it in what garners the most attention. In relation to that, we speak on the broad acceptance of overweight body types while ignoring the risks associated with obese people and all in the name of identity politics. On the other side of the spectrum we talk through the want to be in a committed relationship but the fear of rejection impeding that desire. Additionally, we discuss the feeling of being stagnant in your career while watching your counterparts succeed. As we wrap up the show Tim reveals to use at what age he became aware of his sexuality and why the feeling of anxiety while performing not only never goes away, but why it never should.

item.title: Fahim Anwar
item.description: Today on Unlicensed Therapy I am joined by comedian and host of the podcast “Fahim Anwar Dance Hour”, Fahim Anwar. On this episode Fahim and I begin with covering the basics of what should be required when doing a show or going on a podcast. We also deconstruct what Jay-Z’s 99 problems could possibly be along with why phones have such a bad reputation, especially around friends and partners. Eventually, we come to the conclusion that having the ability to sit in silence while on your phone resembles a deeper connection. As we reach the premise of the show Fahim shares his first world problems such as difficulty balancing career and personal life. Though, his biggest disappointment lies in the entertainment industry, most being from commercial auditioning but all auditions nonetheless. Stay with us as we go deeper on the details here on Unlicensed Therapy!

item.title: Rick Glassman
item.description: Today on the podcast we are joined by comedian and actor Rick Glassman. On this episode Rick shares with us how being diagnosed with Asperger syndrome late in life affected both his professional and personal life. Growing up Rick lacked the ability to read social and facial ques and as a result had little to no friends. Recognizing these limitations proved to be the easiest hurdle. Learning how to navigate through this condition on the other hand showed to be more challenging. As a high functioning adult Rick has been able to adapt almost effortlessly but not without the help of comedy. Comedy has directly improved his ability to connect with people through validation and although it is an ongoing battle his stage persona has formed into something you would not recognize from his young self. As we go further into the show Rick shares with us a few anecdotes confronting a security guard along with his basketball aspirations in college.

item.title: Rafinha Bastos
item.description: Today on Unlicensed Therapy I interview comedian, actor, and television personality Rafinha Bastos. Rafinha stated his career in Brazil as a host for multiple TV shows until he became noticed worldwide after The New York Times named him the most influential person on Twitter. Shortly after opportunities came pouring in and it was through that avenue that Rafinha was able to eventually pursue comedy in English. On this episode we discuss how different not just comedy is in Brazil compared to America but how different the culture is too. Being born in Brazil, Rafinha grew up with much friendlier customs on how people, including strangers, treated each other. As oppose to in America where a bystander will more likely than not take no notice of your presence. Recognizing this contrast plays into how comedy is perceived in each country. In America audience members are more likely to become offended whereas in Brazil they are more accepting of jokes. As we continue the conversation Rafinha shares with us his experience of having a child who lives in a different country while pursuing his career and coming to the realization that he may be overprioritizing sex in his relationships.

item.title: Joel Jimenez
item.description: Today on Unlicensed Therapy I speak with comedian Joel Jimenez to discuss his constant state of anxiety and attempt to mitigate it. From a young age Joel has experienced major panic and anxiety issues which have only been magnified by his mother’s ongoing health problems. Living in such high levels of panic have now resulted in developing insomnia, sometimes only sleeping 3 hours a night. In addition to living in fear, the remnant of his illness has even given people secondhand anxiety. Taking into account that Joel has no health insurance we take this opportunity to approach new avenues for possible remedies, experimenting with homosexuality or meditating serve as a couple examples. As we go on in the episode Joel shares with us how he suffers from hypochondria and his perspective as to why his children will not be introduced to Santa Claus.

item.title: Brody Stevens
item.description:
Today on Unlicensed Therapy, I am joined by comedian Brody Stevens. Brody started off his career in Los Angeles by taking a crash course in comedy before performing at monotonous open mics. Already disillusioned by the prospect of following this path into comedy, he was approached Danny Robinson who advised him to leave LA and find a smaller market. Having relatives in Seattle the decision to move there was quick and soon after Brody started doing public access on live cable. Although his following was beginning to grow the idea of becoming a T.V. personality rather than a stand-up comic was not what he envisioned so for this reason the decision to move to New York was made.
On this episode Brody shares with us his experience in dealing with mental illness along with the various amount of medications that are prescribed. Never having the need to seek psychiatric help in his young years, Brody found himself being faced with the contradictory benefits of feeling “normal” but at the price of limited creativity. After numerous attempts of gradually weaning himself off of medications he finally found a balance while seeking home remedies but never dismissing the idea of going back. Make sure to stay with us until the end as Brody reveals his police encounter at Starbucks!

item.title: JT Parr
item.description:
Today on Unlicensed Therapy I speak with JT Parr he is a comedian, activist and current co-host on Chad Goes Deep. On this episode JT walks us through his experience in overcoming two forms of addiction; Adderall and webcam porn. Prior to becoming clean JT was spending massive amounts of his parent’s money on his vices while simultaneously feeling high levels of anxiety and adrenaline which subsequently translated into suicidal thoughts. The tipping point finally came when JT learned that his parents no longer had any trust in him which then included them cutting him off financially.
Fast forward to today JT is an activist for bleaching of the coral reef and has been consistent in staying clean. As we go through the show JT reveals to us his sensitive side and how that contributed to his father possibly believing that he is gay. Stay with us to learn what exactly sparked that idea.
